value.content:
Spent an afternoon last week debugging an Azure pipeline that 'worked locally'.

The fix took 4 minutes. The diagnosis took 3 hours.

Here's the checklist I'd give past-me — saves you the same loop. 🧵

1/ The pipeline yaml resolves variables at *queue time*, not run time.
If you reference $(Build.SourceBranch) in a variable group, it freezes to the branch that triggered the queue — not the one running now.

2/ `condition:` doesn't short-circuit downstream stages cleanly. A stage that's `Skipped` still inherits artifacts from the previous one — but only the ones declared, not the implicit checkout. Bit me twice this month.
3/ Service connection scope is per-project, not per-pipeline. If you grant it 'Owner' to debug once, it stays Owner forever. Audit those quarterly.

Full writeup + the diagnostic snippet I now run first on every broken pipeline: ventrue.tech/blog/azure-pipeline-debug-checklist

What's the worst pipeline bug you've shipped to prod?
